methods.description: This method detects consumption biases towards different-aged pollen stores by comparing observed consumption rates with expected rates under random consumption using chi-square tests.

steps.description: Consumption biases towards different-aged pollen stores were detected by comparing the observed consumption rate with the rate expected under random consumption.
steps.searchable_summary: compare observed and expected consumption rates using chi-square test

steps.description: The overall distribution of emptied and unemptied cells across pollen-age classes was compared against the numbers expected by random emptying alone by a chi-square test of independence.
steps.searchable_summary: perform chi-square test of independence on cell distribution
inputs: distribution of emptied and unemptied cells

steps.description: Post-hoc pairwise comparisons of emptied and unemptied cell distributions between pollen-age class were examined with a chi-square test of independence.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct post-hoc pairwise chi-square tests on cell distributions

steps.description: Pairwise comparisons were made with 2 x 2 contingency tables with emptied/unemptied cells as one variable and pollen-age class as the other variable.
steps.searchable_summary: create 2x2 contingency tables for pairwise comparisons

steps.description: Chi-square critical values were compared against a Bonferroni-corrected significance level (p = 0.05/number of pairwise comparisons (21), or 0.00238) to correct for the large number of pairwise comparisons.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Bonferroni correction to chi-square critical values

methods.method_name: Dual Choice and No Choice Assays
methods.description: This method compares the amounts of each pollen source consumed by caged bees using paired t-tests and analyzes various metrics across pollen treatments using ANOVA.

steps.description: In the dual choice assays, the amounts of each pollen source consumed by the caged bees were compared by a paired t-test.
steps.searchable_summary: perform paired t-test on pollen source consumption

steps.description: In the no choice performance assays, adult worker body mass, pollen consumption (fresh mass consumed), dried hindgut contents, and HPG protein titers were compared across pollen treatments by a one-way ANOVA.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct one-way ANOVA on various metrics across pollen treatments

steps.description: Means from significant comparisons were compared by a post-hoc Tukey's test.
steps.searchable_summary: apply post-hoc Tukey's test to significant ANOVA results

methods.description: This method tests for normality of consumption and bee development metrics using Shapiro-Wilk, Kolmogorov-Smirnov tests, and normality probability plots.

steps.description: All consumption and bee development metrics were tested for normality with the Shapiro-Wilk test, Kolmogorov-Smirnov test, and by examination of normality probability plots.
steps.searchable_summary: test normality using Shapiro-Wilk, Kolmogorov-Smirnov, and probability plots
